mnemonics: FLAT is simple, NESTED is like building blocks. A flat transaction is a single block; a nested transaction is a parent block with smaller child blocks inside it.
keyPoints: A flat transaction is a single, atomic unit of work with a single begin and end.
A nested transaction allows transactions to be composed of other subtransactions, creating a hierarchy.
Only the top-level (root) transaction in a nested structure can commit; aborting a parent transaction forces all its children to abort.
commonMistakes: Assuming a committed subtransaction in a nested structure is permanently committed (it's only permanent if the root commits).
Confusing nested transactions with distributed transactions (nested refers to structure, distributed refers to execution across multiple nodes).
